content:
# Matthew Chapter 8 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_008_page_001.jpg, MAT_008_page_002.jpg

### Healing a Man with Leprosy

1. When Jesus came down from the mountain, vast crowds followed him.

2. A man with leprosy approached and knelt before him. "Lord," he said, "if you are willing, you can make me clean."

3. Jesus reached out and touched him. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean." Instantly the leprosy disappeared.

4. Then Jesus told him, "Don't tell anyone. Instead, go show yourself to the priest and present the offering Moses prescribed — that will be your testimony to them."

### The Centurion's Faith

5. When Jesus entered Capernaum, a Roman centurion came to him with an urgent request.

6. "Lord," he said, "my servant is lying at home paralyzed, in terrible agony."

7. Jesus said, "I will come and heal him."

8. But the centurion replied, "Lord, I don't deserve to have you come into my home. Just say the word from here, and my servant will be healed.

9. I understand authority — I'm a man under orders myself, with soldiers under me. I tell one, 'Go,' and he goes. I tell another, 'Come,' and he comes. I tell my servant, 'Do this,' and he does it."

10. When Jesus heard this, he was amazed. He turned to the crowd following him and said, "I tell you the truth — I have not found faith like this in all of Israel!

11. And I tell you that many will come from the east and the west and take their places at the feast with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.

12. But those who were born into the kingdom will be thrown out into utter darkness, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth."

13. Then Jesus said to the centurion, "Go. What you believed would happen has happened." And his servant was healed at that very hour.

### Many Healings

14. When Jesus arrived at Peter's house, he found Peter's mother-in-law in bed with a fever.

15. He touched her hand, and the fever vanished. She got up and began serving them.

16. That evening, people brought to him many who were tormented by demons. He drove out the spirits with a single command and healed everyone who was sick.

17. This fulfilled the word of the prophet Isaiah: "He took our weaknesses upon himself and carried our diseases."

### The Cost of Following Jesus

18. When Jesus saw the crowd pressing around him, he gave orders to cross to the other side of the lake.

19. A religious scholar came up to him and said, "Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go."

20. Jesus replied, "Foxes have dens. Birds have nests. But the Son of Man has no place to call home."

21. Another man, one of his disciples, said, "Lord, first let me go home and bury my father."

22. But Jesus told him, "Follow me — and let the dead bury their own dead."

### Jesus Calms the Storm

23. Then he got into the boat, and his disciples followed him.

24. Without warning, a violent storm erupted on the lake — waves crashing over the boat. But Jesus was sleeping.

25. The disciples rushed to wake him. "Lord, save us! We're going to drown!"

26. He said to them, "Why are you so afraid? Where is your faith?" Then he stood up and commanded the wind and the waves to stop. And it became completely calm.

27. The disciples were awestruck. "What kind of man is this?" they said. "Even the wind and the waves do what he says!"

### The Gadarene Demoniacs

28. When he reached the other side, in the region of the Gadarenes, two men who were possessed by demons came out from among the tombs to confront him. They were so violent that no one dared travel that road.

29. They screamed, "What do you want with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us ahead of schedule?"

30. Some distance away, a large herd of pigs was feeding.

31. The demons begged him, "If you're going to cast us out, send us into that herd of pigs."

32. "Go!" he said. So they came out and entered the pigs — and the entire herd stampeded down the cliff into the lake and drowned.

33. The men tending the pigs ran to the town and reported everything, including what had happened to the demon-possessed men.

34. Then the whole town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their region.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 2 — "touched him":** Lepers were untouchable in Jewish law. Jesus's first act is physical contact — not a word from a distance, but a hand laid on diseased flesh. The touch precedes the healing and is itself a radical statement.

**Verse 10 — "I have not found faith like this in all of Israel":** The irony is sharp: the greatest faith Jesus has encountered belongs to a Roman military officer — a Gentile, an occupier. Matthew is making a point about who recognizes Jesus's authority.

**Verse 20 — "no place to call home":** The Son of Man title (from Daniel 7) evokes a figure of cosmic authority — yet this figure is homeless. The contrast between the title's grandeur and the reality of itinerant poverty is deliberately jarring.

**Verse 22 — "let the dead bury their own dead":** One of Jesus's most shocking statements. Burying one's father was the most sacred obligation in Jewish culture. Jesus's demand overrides even this — the call to follow him is absolute. "The dead" who bury "the dead" — the spiritually dead can handle the physically dead.

**Verse 24 — "violent storm" for σεισμὸς μέγας:** Matthew's word (σεισμός) literally means "earthquake" — as if the sea itself convulsed. This is not just weather. Matthew uses the same word for the earthquake at Jesus's death (27:54). The language suggests cosmic disturbance.

**Verse 29 — "ahead of schedule":** The demons say πρὸ καιροῦ — "before the time." They know there is an appointed time for their judgment and destruction. Jesus's arrival forces the question: has the schedule moved up?

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
